label: Nonlinear activations

node.label: Nonlinear activations
node.definition: Elementwise or gated nonlinearities let stacked layers approximate nonlinear functions.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Nonlinear activations is to connect Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Affine transforms & dense layers to downstream capabilities including Boolean circuits & differentiable logic gates, Gating & multiplicative control, Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers.
examples: substrate for Boolean circuits & differentiable logic gates
substrate for Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Affine transforms & dense layers.

dependencies.rationale: Nonlinear activations uses Calculus & automatic differentiation theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Nonlinear activations is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
